q1: What is your overall experience with badminton and other racket sports?
q1a: I have never played badminton or any other racket sport.
q1b: I have a background in another racket sport but I'm new to badminton.
q1c: I've played badminton a few times and know the very basics.
q1d: I play badminton regularly and feel comfortable on court.
q2: How well can you clear and lift from the back court?
q2a: I struggle to hit clears over the net or keep them in bounds.
q2b: I can hit basic clears and lifts slowly but lack depth and control.
q2c: I clear and lift with reasonable depth and can recover after defending.
q2d: I use clears and lifts to reset rallies and change pace with control.
q3: How comfortable are you with net play (taps, blocks, and attacking shots)?
q3a: I avoid the net or only react when the shuttle comes to me.
q3b: I can play simple net shots but struggle with tight spins or fast exchanges.
q3c: I play net shots confidently and can finish points when the chance appears.
q3d: I control the net with tight shots, deception, and quick follow-ups.
q4: How well do you handle doubles rotation and court positioning?
q4a: I'm not sure when to rotate or who covers which area in doubles.
q4b: I try to rotate with my partner but often end up in the wrong position.
q4c: I rotate and cover front/back with my partner in most rallies.
q4d: I switch smoothly between attack and defense and cover the court as a unit.
q5: How would you describe your match play and knowledge of badminton rules?
q5a: I'm still learning scoring, service rules, and doubles service order.
q5b: I know basic rules but mostly react without a clear plan.
q5c: I understand service courts, lets, and common doubles tactics.
q5d: I'm comfortable with match format, service rotation, and adjusting tactics during play.
